Throughout my professional journey, I have been dedicated to open source advocacy, technical education, and community leadership, with a focus on bridging the gap between global cloud-native advancements and local practitioners. Notably, I have taken a leading role in organizing KCD Seoul and have served as an organizer for the Cloud Native Community Groups (Seoul), effectively acting as an ambassador for CNCF within Korea. Through these activities, I have empowered Korean developers and organizations to fully leverage CNCF projects by providing timely, accessible, and high-quality technical content, workshops, and mentorship programs. I am committed to reducing language and cultural barriers by localizing key resources, fostering inclusive community spaces, and encouraging active participation in global CNCF initiatives. As a CNCF Ambassador, I aim to be a trusted liaison, ensuring that the Korean community not only keeps pace with global trends but also contributes meaningfully to the evolution of the cloud-native ecosystem.

person.bio: Key Researcher & Lecturer, Public Speaker, Creator of the Software Bill of Behavior (SBoB) and maintainer of the Kubernetes Storm Center for cloudnative intelligence Backstory: Living intentionally is hard when routine takes control. I grew up wanting to be a scientist, studying Biochemistry, starting med school, and ultimately earning a PhD in General Relativity to explore how humans perceive 'reality.' When US funding cuts ended that path, I realized academia left me without agency.In the private sector, I discovered the value of risk. The Cloud Native paradigm let me experiment, fail fast, and try again without fear of lasting damage. In positive-sum collaborations, I saw how my algorithms could shape real-world outcomes, and how taking calculated risks created visible change for people and systems alike. This freedom to test ideas with relatively low cost revealed a new kind of agency, an immediacy and measurable connection to the meaning of work.My vision is to empower others to leverage cloud native technology with respect for the human element, security, and risk. Because having agency means creating reality for our society, for each one of us, one epsilon at a time.

person.bio: I'm a DevOps with a programming background. In my free time, I enjoy learning new tech stuff. I'm driven by designing and implementing complete systems that are both optimized and satisfy stakeholder needs.

person.name: Gnani Rahul Nutakki
person.bio: I am a Principal Cloud Platform Engineer at Radiant Logic with over eight years of experience in cloud automation, Kubernetes, Terraform, and modern infrastructure design. I hold multiple Kubernetes and AWS certifications, focusing on building secure, scalable, and efficient platform systems across multi-cloud environments. My work addresses critical challenges in agentic AI security, discovery, and management. I am also passionate about cloud-native technologies and continuous learning, regularly sharing insights through on my Medium publication Clear-Text.

person.name: Lukasz Gryglicki
person.bio:
Lukasz has been a Senior Developer at the CNCF since 2017 and has been working for The Linux Foundation since 2018. He is the creator and maintainer of the DevStats reporting tool. Prior to joining the CNCF, Lukasz worked remotely for companies based in the US, including Cleverstep, Jamis and Spreemo Health.

He loves to travel in polar areas, such as polar Norway, Finland and Russia. From 2011 to 2012, he was a scientist for a Polish polar expedition to the Hornsund fjord on the island of Spitsbergen, in northern Norway. Lukasz graduated from Warsaw University of Technology with a Master of Science in Engineering. He lives in a small town in Poland with his wife and two kids.

person.name: Mauricio Fernandes
person.bio: Mauricio Fernandes is a Network DevOps Architect with 20+ years of experience designing and operating large-scale cloud, data center, and enterprise network platforms. He currently leads automation-first and AI-ready network initiatives at Marriott, applying Infrastructure as Code, SRE practices, and data-driven operations to improve reliability and scale. Previously, he served as a Senior Solutions Architect at AWS, where he partnered with customers on cloud-native and AI/ML architectures. He is passionate about using automation and AI to transform infrastructure into resilient, self-service platforms.

person.name: Tomáš Kubla
person.bio: 🔐 Security specialist who fell in love with cloud-native world.🌍 Currently spreading my roots in the Netherlands — not just in tech, but also in the 🧗 bouldering, 💃 lindy hop, and 👞 tap dance community.💡 Motto: "If describing an issue in an open-source project takes you too longer, just fix it."

person.name: Amro Elamin
person.bio: A cloud architect that’s enthusiastic about container orchestration and open source cloud-native technologies.

person.name: Emídio Neto
person.bio: Emídio is a Site Reliability Engineer. He holds a Master's degree in Computer Science and has experience in software and infrastructure development, working on the conceptualization, design, and building of scalable solutions and architectures for cloud-native CPaaS/SaaS industry applications. Besides coding, Emídio has experience as a teacher, and he loves diving into local community projects and teaching. Emídio is also the Chapter Lead of the Cloud Native Community Group in RN/Brazil.

person.name: Ilsun Choi
person.bio: I am a Cloud and IT Security Specialist with over 10 years of hands-on experience in corporate training and consulting. My specialties center on CNCF technologies like Kubernetes, cloud security across multi-cloud environments (AWS, Azure), and implementing DevSecOps principles and infrastructure automation. I have a proven track record of delivering professional training to engineers at major corporations such as Samsung SDS, LG CNS, and Nexon. You can find my insights and articles on my blog at "https://gasbugs.tistory.com".

person.name: Samuel Holmes
person.bio: I am a Software/Platform engineer, driven by curiosity and innovation, that I use to create meaningful, impactful solutions for society and the environment. I am passionate about many various subjects, from music to cooking, with, of course, Kubernetes and IT in the middle.

person.name: Sergey Tsveiman
person.bio: I’m passionate about cloud technologies, machine learning, and automation.

person.name: Tjandy
person.bio: Tjandy is a software engineer with experience in AI platform services and distributed systems. He takes a practical, hands-on approach, preferring to delve into technologies directly to understand how they work. Tjandy consistently explores new concepts and technologies, believing that staying current through continuous learning is essential in today's fast-changing tech landscape. His work centers on building reliable, effective systems that solve real problems and make a positive difference for users.

person.name: Damien Obringer
person.bio: Damien is an experienced Solutions Architect currently working at PwC Luxembourg. He is passionate about designing and implementing scalable, highly available, and maintainable applications using .NET, Kubernetes, Helm, and other Cloud-Native technologies.

person.name: Jon Zeolla
person.bio: Jon Zeolla is the founder of Zenable, where they are revolutionizing corporate governance by integrating cloud-native security and compliance with artificial intelligence. Previously he co-founded Seiso where he received the 2021 Start-Up Innovator of the Year Award and currently serves as strategic advisor, guiding key business decisions and company growth.He is also a CNCF Ambassador, SANS Instructor for SEC540: Cloud Security and DevSecOps Automation, and an active member of CNCF TAG Security.

person.name: Md Arif Hossen
person.bio: Arif is a Cloud, Platform, Cybersecurity, and AI Architect with over two decade of experience in telecom and cloud-native transformation. He is a passionate advocate for open source and community-driven innovation. As a community leader, Arif co-organizes community activity with Kubernetes User Group Bangladesh (K8SUGBD) and Cloud Native Rajshahi, fostering collaboration and knowledge-sharing across the region.

person.name: Ansari Eramangalath Mohamed Yousuf
person.bio: I'm a dedicated Cloud Architect specializing in EKS architecture and Kubernetes solutions across multi-cloud environments, including AWS, Azure (AKS), and Google Cloud (GKE). My hands-on experience spans the full Kubernetes lifecycle, from greenfield deployments using kubeadm to leveraging Azure Arc for hybrid management. I actively contribute to the CNCF ecosystem in LinkedIn, focusing on practical cloud-native adoption.

person.name: Hari Krishna Sigicherla
person.bio: I’m a Cloud Devops engineer with 12years of IT experiences. I specialized in cloud platforms(AWS/AZURE), provisioning infrastructure to managing cloud-native application deployments. I've good exposure to Cloud, CI/CD, Security & Observability working on various roles & responsibilities. Certified in AWS, AWS, Kubernetes, Terraform & Databricks. Excited to explore the new opportunities as a CNCF ambassador.

person.name: Jan Buijnsters
person.bio: Two years ago I started working as a DevOps engineer, after completing my Computer Science and Engineering MSc degree at TU Delft. During my studies, I developed a strong interest in distributed systems and embarked on my Kubernetes learning journey. Over the past two years, I have begun contributing to various open-source projects and am eager to expand my involvement with CNCF projects, I am taking a particular interest in the FluxCD project. I look forward to participating in future CNCF meetups and taking a more active role in the community, sharing knowledge and collaborating with fellow cloud-native enthusiasts.
